spec_trace.roadmap_pointer: Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.144.md 7.6.3 (Phase 1) -> [ADD v02.144] Second-pass feature-family coverage / coverage sweep

draft_scope.intent:
- What: Backfill Docs & Sheets runtime identity, governed operations, and operator-visible evidence across structured editing surfaces.
- Why: Docs & Sheets are core creative/productivity surfaces and future model/tool routing depends on deterministic coverage instead of prose-only intent.
draft_scope.scope_sketch:
- IN_SCOPE:
  - Docs & Sheets runtime mappings for jobs, tool surfaces, and provenance updates.
  - DCC / Flight Recorder visibility for structured editing and formula operations.
- OUT_OF_SCOPE:
  - Full editor implementation.
  - Broad style/polish work.
draft_scope.acceptance_criteria:
- Docs & Sheets have explicit runtime and operator visibility mapping.
- Appendix rows make future tool routing deterministic for local/cloud models.
draft_scope.risks_unknowns: - Risk: Docs/Sheets remain important but appendix-thin, causing later models to guess capabilities.

assumptions_to_attack: A stub is planning metadata, not execution authority.
Stub Markdown may contain stale draft scope until activated through a signed packet.
Apps must not treat stub contracts as runnable work packets.
minimum_controls: Require official packet contract before Coder or Validator launch.
Fail drift checks when stub Markdown changes without contract regeneration.
